segments.text: Moreover the Lord answered Job, and said:
Shall the one who contends with the Almighty correct Him? He who rebukes God, let him answer it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then Job answered the Lord and said:
Behold, I am vile; What shall I answer You? I lay my hand over my mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Once I have spoken, but I will not answer; Yes, twice, but I will proceed no further.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said:
Now prepare yourself like a man; I will question you, and you shall answer Me:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Would you indeed annul My judgment? Would you condemn Me that you may be justified?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Have you an arm like God? Or can you thunder with a voice like His?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then adorn yourself with majesty and splendor, And array yourself with glory and beauty.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Disperse the rage of your wrath; Look on everyone who is proud, and humble him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Look on everyone who is proud, and bring him low; Tread down the wicked in their place.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hide them in the dust together, Bind their faces in hidden darkness.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then I will also confess to you That your own right hand can save you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Look now at the behemoth, which I made along with you; He eats grass like an ox.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: See now, his strength is in his hips, And his power is in his stomach muscles.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He moves his tail like a cedar; The sinews of his thighs are tightly knit.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: His bones are like beams of bronze, His ribs like bars of iron.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He is the first of the ways of God; Only He who made him can bring near His sword.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Surely the mountains yield food for him, And all the beasts of the field play there.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He lies under the lotus trees, In a covert of reeds and marsh.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The lotus trees cover him with their shade; The willows by the brook surround him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Indeed the river may rage, Yet he is not disturbed; He is confident, though the Jordan gushes into his mouth,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Though he takes it in his eyes, Or one pierces his nose with a snare.
segments.speaker: God
